hero.subtitle: Bridging the Governance Gap in Digital Trade & Sustainable Development. Build AI tools that transform how governments monitor complex regulations.

about.title: AI for Global Impact
about.subtitle: A strategic initiative by UNESCAP and KMITL to solve real-world challenges using sustainable AI and regulatory technology.
about.mission_desc: Address the critical "Governance Gap" facing developing economies in the Asia-Pacific by promoting AI-assisted regulatory analysis to strengthen how governments monitor and respond to complex digital trade regulations.
about.partnership_desc: UNESCAP brings policy leadership and international development networks. KMITL provides technical infrastructure, research leadership, and access to the innovation ecosystem needed to translate AI methods into practical solutions.
about.vision_desc: Shift from analog, reactive regulatory oversight to proactive, AI-assisted monitoring. Democratize governance so developing nations in Asia-Pacific can audit their regulatory status independently.
about.capacity_desc: A strategic investment in human capacity, bridging the gap between code and law. We equip a new generation of multidisciplinary professionals to tackle real-world regulatory challenges.

challenge.title: The Governance Gap
challenge.speed_title: Speed of Trade vs. Speed of Law
challenge.speed_desc: Digital data flows exceed 50 TB/s while national policy adaptation cycles remain trapped in a manual, 20th-century update process spanning years or decades.
challenge.shredded_desc: Regulatory data is locked in unstructured formats. Standard AI treats it as plain text, destroying logical hierarchy and leading to legal hallucinations.
challenge.objective_desc: Build a modular AI tool (Automated Regulatory Engine) that intelligently identifies, classifies, and summarizes digital trade regulatory provisions.
challenge.mod_a_desc: Detect passages with accurate source-grounded citations.
challenge.mod_b_desc: Assign provisions to appropriate policy categories.
challenge.mod_c_desc: Generate concise summaries for non-technical officials.

timeline.title: Your Roadmap to the Finale
timeline.step1_desc: Onboard talent and set technical benchmarks. Round 1 open call begins.
timeline.step2_desc: Selection of the Top 20 teams based on technical functionality.
timeline.step3_desc: Virtual mentoring and online workshops with UN/KMITL experts.
timeline.step5_desc: Onsite 3-hour 'Live Hack' and pitch demonstration of winning prototypes.

prizes.title: What's at Stake
prizes.ben1_desc: Direct guidance from UN experts and top academic faculty to shape your real-world solution.
prizes.ben2_desc: Directly contribute to global Sustainable Development Goals through functional technology.
prizes.ben3_desc: Gain immense exposure to the cutting edge of RegTech policies across the Asia-Pacific region.

teams.title: Where Code Meets Law
teams.subtitle: Teams must be multidisciplinary and consist of at least 3 members. You need a blend of technical and policy expertise.
teams.tech_desc: AI/ML Architecture, Python, Data Processing. Handle data scraping, HtmlRAG, and model orchestration.
teams.policy_desc: Trade Law, Economics, Regulatory Frameworks. Ensure solutions address real policy needs and accurate legal interpretation.
teams.members_desc: Multidisciplinary teams are required. Open to students and developers worldwide.

apply.title: Round 1: Global Open Call
apply.subtitle: In Round 1, prototype development is not required. To accept the challenge, your team must submit:
apply.pitch_desc: Clearly present the problem, your proposed RegTech solution, use of AI/LLMs, and expected impact.
apply.video_desc: Explain the problem context and your solution concept. Showcase your team and their expertise.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: For Round 1 (Global Open Call), you only need to submit a 5-slide pitch deck (PDF) and a 7-minute video presentation. No working prototype is required at this stage. For later rounds, you will need your development setup and any tools needed for your AI pipeline.
faq.a2: Yes, participation in the RegTech Structural Intelligence AI Hackathon is completely free. There are no registration fees or hidden costs.
faq.q3: Is food provided during the onsite event?
faq.a3: Yes, for the Grand Finale onsite event in September 2026, full catering will be provided for all participants throughout the event.
faq.a4: HtmlRAG is an approach that preserves the structural integrity of HTML documents during AI processing. Instead of stripping HTML to plain text (which destroys logical hierarchy, nested clauses, and table logic), HtmlRAG maintains the document structure to produce more accurate and reliable AI outputs.
faq.a5: The hackathon is open to students, developers, policy researchers, and professionals worldwide. Teams must be multidisciplinary with at least 3 members, combining technical expertise (AI/ML, Python) and policy expertise (trade law, economics).
faq.q6: What are Pillars 6 and 7 of the RDTII?
faq.a6: The Regional Digital Trade Integration Index (RDTII) measures digital trade readiness. Pillar 6 covers Cross-Border Data Policies (data localization, ban & local processing, conditional flow regimes) and Pillar 7 covers Domestic Data Protection & Privacy (legal frameworks, data retention, compliance costs).

footer.title: Using AI for Digital Trade Regulatory Analysis
